$comment: Sidecar contract (NOT a Claude Code hook file). It records that hooks/hooks.json ships intentionally empty. Kept separate from hooks/hooks.json so the Claude plugin hook schema stays minimal/unpolluted. The validator (scripts/validate_plugin.py) asserts this file exists and is consistent whenever hooks/hooks.json is the empty placeholder, so an empty array can never be silently read as a regression. See issue #6.
rationale: Enabled-by-default PreToolUse gates change the user's runtime behavior (blocking/rewriting tool calls). This plugin must not mutate that behavior without an explicit opt-in, so no default state-mutating hook is shipped. The metadata-first / plan-before-fetch discipline is enforced through prose (skill + commands), per-CLI --allowedTools scoping, and the docs below. A stronger enabled-by-default gate remains a maintainer/product decision; issue #6 stays open for it.
